# wellplayed_custom_field_definition (Resource)

Manages a WellPlayed custom field definition attached to an object type (Tournament, Account, Team, etc.) within the organization. Backed by the `createCustomFieldDefinition`, `updateCustomFieldDefinition`, and `deleteCustomFieldDefinition` mutations.

Custom field *values* (per-object data filled in by users) are not managed by this resource — only the definition itself. The identity fields `object_type`, `key`, and `type` are immutable; changing any of them forces replacement.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A free-text custom field attached to every tournament in the organization.
resource "wellplayed_custom_field_definition" "tournament_sponsor" {
  object_type = "Tournament"
  key         = "sponsor"
  name        = "Sponsor"
  description = "Headline sponsor displayed on the tournament page."

  type        = "STRING"
  required    = false
  visibility  = "PUBLIC"
  editability = "ALWAYS"
}

# A required single-select field on player accounts, with a fixed set of
# options. SELECT/MULTI_SELECT fields are the only ones that read `options`.
resource "wellplayed_custom_field_definition" "account_region" {
  object_type = "Account"
  key         = "region"
  name        = "Region"

  type        = "SELECT"
  required    = true
  visibility  = "PUBLIC"
  editability = "ONE_TIME"

  options = [
    { label = "Europe", value = "EU" },
    { label = "North America", value = "NA" },
    { label = "Asia", value = "AS" },
  ]
}

# A regex-validated string only visible to staff with the right permission.
resource "wellplayed_custom_field_definition" "account_internal_id" {
  object_type = "Account"
  key         = "internal_id"
  name        = "Internal ID"
  description = "Internal CRM identifier."

  type             = "STRING"
  visibility       = "WITH_PERMISSION"
  editability      = "WITH_PERMISSION"
  unique           = true
  validation_regex = "^[A-Z]{2}-\\d{6}$"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `editability` (String) Editability rule controlling when and by whom this field can be modified. One of `ALWAYS`, `ONE_TIME`, `WITH_PERMISSION`.
- `key` (String) Unique key identifier for this field within the object type. Used as the stable lookup name when reading or writing values. Immutable.
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name of the field.
- `object_type` (String) Object type this field is attached to (e.g. `Tournament`, `Account`, `Team`, `Organization`). See the `ObjectType` enum in the WellPlayed schema for the full list. Immutable.
- `type` (String) Data type of the field value. One of `STRING`, `NUMBER`, `BOOLEAN`, `DATE`, `EMAIL`, `PHONE`, `URL`, `COUNTRY`, `SELECT`, `MULTI_SELECT`, `IMAGE`. Immutable.
- `visibility` (String) Visibility level controlling who can view this field value. One of `PUBLIC`, `OWNER`, `OWNER_OR_PERMISSION`, `WITH_PERMISSION`.

### Optional

- `default_value` (String) Default value applied when no value is provided, serialized as a string.
- `description` (String) Optional description explaining the purpose of this field.
- `options` (Attributes List) Available options for `SELECT` or `MULTI_SELECT` fields. Ignored for other types. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--options))
- `order` (Number) Display order position of this field. Assigned by the server when omitted.
- `required` (Boolean) Whether this field must be filled in when creating/updating the parent object. Defaults to `false` on the server when omitted.
- `unique` (Boolean) Whether the value must be unique across all objects of this type. Defaults to `false` on the server when omitted.
- `validation_regex` (String) Optional regex pattern the value must match.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the definition was created (RFC 3339).
- `id` (String) Unique identifier of the custom field definition.
- `updated_at` (String) When the definition was last updated (RFC 3339).

<a id="nestedatt--options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `options`

Required:

- `label` (String) Human-readable label shown to the user.
- `value` (String) Stable value persisted when the option is selected.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Custom field definitions are imported by "<object_type>:<id>" — the object
# type is required because the API has no single-record lookup.
terraform import wellplayed_custom_field_definition.tournament_sponsor Tournament:cfd_01h...
```
